hypothesis: If SAM-EM is fine-tuned on synthetic LPTEM videos with box-prompt conditioning and evaluated on diverse test scenarios (varying particle counts, overlaps, and resolutions), then it will outperform a U‑Net baseline in segmentation accuracy (IoU, F1, J&F) and provide centroid localizations more aligned with manual annotations, because the training data expose it to realistic variability in particle configurations and SNR conditions.
